title: Django raw SQL string concatenation — SQL injection
evidence: cursor.execute("SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE name = '" + q + "'")
notes: User-controlled request.GET['q'] concatenated into raw SQL; reachable on unauthenticated GET /search.
triage_notes: Definite SQLi: unauthenticated request.GET['q'] concatenated straight into cursor.execute — no FP pattern applies.

title: Django template |safe on user input — reflected XSS
fix_recipe:
from django.utils.html import format_html, escape
comment = escape(request.POST["comment"])
# Or use format_html for structured HTML construction
notes: q comes from request.GET and is piped through |safe, disabling auto-escape.
triage_notes: q is request.GET['q'] per search.py:6; |safe disables auto-escape on attacker-controlled input.

title: Django template |safe on user-influenced row data — stored/reflected XSS
evidence:
{% for r in rows %}
  • {{ r.1|safe }}
    • {% endfor %}
fix_recipe:
from django.utils.html import format_html, escape
comment = escape(request.POST["comment"])
# Or use format_html for structured HTML construction
notes: Rendering DB-row user names with |safe allows stored XSS if names contain HTML.
triage_notes: Row data from users table rendered with |safe; DB content is not trusted for HTML output.

title: Django DEBUG=True in settings
notes: DEBUG=True in what appears to be the only settings file; exposes stack traces and local variables.
triage_notes: File is app/settings.py (not settings_dev.py) and is the sole settings module — FP pattern does not apply.

title: Django hardcoded SECRET_KEY
notes: Hardcoded static SECRET_KEY allows session forgery and signed-cookie attacks.
triage_notes: Hardcoded string literal in production-path settings.py; no CI guard present.

title: nginx weak TLS protocols (TLSv1 and TLSv1.1 enabled)
notes: RFC 9325 deprecates TLSv1.0 and TLSv1.1; POODLE/BEAST exposure.
triage_notes: Directive affirmatively enables TLSv1 and TLSv1.1 (not a disable/exclude list).

title: nginx server_tokens on — version disclosure
notes: server_tokens on emits nginx version in Server header/error pages.
triage_notes: Explicit server_tokens on in production vhost — unambiguous info leak.

title: nginx missing HSTS header on TLS server block
notes: No Strict-Transport-Security header present in the TLS vhost.
triage_notes: Full conf read; no HSTS add_header anywhere in file.

title: Dockerfile runs as root — missing USER directive
fix_recipe:
FROM node:20.12.2-alpine3.19@sha256:<digest>
WORKDIR /app
RUN addgroup -S appgroup && adduser -S appuser -G appgroup
COPY --chown=appuser:appgroup package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --omit=dev
COPY --chown=appuser:appgroup . .
USER appuser
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
notes: Container uses default root UID 0; no USER instruction present in Dockerfile.
triage_notes: Single-stage Dockerfile with no USER directive anywhere; final stage runs as root.

title: Dockerfile base image not pinned to digest
notes: Mutable tag python:3.11 can drift silently; pin to immutable @sha256 digest.
triage_notes: Mutable tag with no @sha256 digest on the only FROM line.

title: Django ALLOWED_HOSTS wildcard
notes: Wildcard ALLOWED_HOSTS accepts any Host header; enables cache-poisoning and password-reset-link poisoning.
triage_notes: Wildcard in production settings.py; commonly intentional in dev but file is not guarded.
